commands.name: vwifi-tool
commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# vwifi-tool --help
 Invalid arguments
 vwifi-tool: A userspace tool which supports more user-specific utilization for vwifi
 
 Usage:
 
 	vwifi-tool [arguments]
 
 The arguments are:
 
 	-d  Destination interface name
 	-s Source interface name
 	-c Clear denylist

binary_metadata.file_type: ELF 64-bit LSB pie executable, x86-64, version 1 (SYSV), dynamically linked, interpreter /lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2, BuildID[sha1]=62fb1a574713f60c39618e1126d52992848d7a79, for GNU/Linux 3.2.0, stripped

apt_metadata.description:
Simple Virtual Wireless Driver for Linux in DKMS format
This package contains a minimal interface to provide essential
functionalities, such as scanning for dummy Wi-Fi networks, establishing
connections, and disconnecting from them.
.
It is built upon the cfg80211 subsystem, which collaborates with FullMAC
drivers. Currently, vwifi supports both Station Mode and Host AP Mode and is
equipped with robust WPA/WPA2 security features. This enables users to
configure a wireless environment using vwifi, hostapd (in HostAP mode
interface), and wpa_supplicant (in station mode interface).

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use vwifi-tool because ### vwifi-dkms.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair vwifi-tool with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `vwifi-tool --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web, wifi_2_4_ghz, wifi_5_ghz] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### vwifi-dkms.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install vwifi-dkms
